applied_optimizations.name: Triton Global Average Pooling Kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel for global average pooling using 2D grid (batch, channel). Block size aligned to AMD wavefront (64 threads). Uses tl.where for masked elements since tl.sum doesn't support mask parameter on this Triton version.

applied_optimizations.name: cudnn.benchmark = True
applied_optimizations.description: Enabled cudnn benchmark mode in ModelNew.__init__ to auto-tune convolution algorithms.

applied_optimizations.name: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high')
applied_optimizations.description: Set high precision for float32 matmul to enable TF32 tensor cores on AMD MI355X.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton Fused BN+ReLU (training mode)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Computing batch statistics correctly in Triton is complex. PyTorch uses biased variance (N) not unbiased (N-1). Multiple attempts had bugs in mean/var computation leading to ~1.0 max diff.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton Fused GAP+FC
attempted_but_failed.reason: Nested loops in Triton (over channels and spatial) caused high kernel launch overhead, resulting in 2.24ms vs 1.76ms baseline

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on forward method
attempted_but_failed.reason: torch.compile conflicts with custom Triton kernels on AMD ROCm. MLIR pass failures: 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' failed to legalize, ImportError for specialize_impl
attempted_but_failed.error: MLIR pass pipeline failure, ConvertTritonAMDGPUToLLVM errors

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on feature_extractor
attempted_but_failed.reason: Same MLIR compatibility issues between torch.compile and Triton kernels

notes: Successfully implemented correct Triton kernel for global average pooling
Model passes all 5 correctness trials with <1e-2 tolerance
Current implementation achieves parity with baseline (0.99x speedup, SCORE: 50)
torch.compile has fundamental compatibility issues with Triton kernels on AMD ROCm - MLIR pass failures
Training-mode BatchNorm in Triton requires careful implementation of biased variance (divide by N, not N-1)
Fused GAP+FC kernel was slower due to nested loop overhead in Triton
Next optimization stage should explore: aiter kernels for Conv+BN+ReLU fusion, or careful use of torch.compile without Triton in compiled region

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output — agent got stuck for the second consecutive trial. With ~34 minutes remaining, providing exact copy-paste code for a pure PyTorch approach (no Triton kernels) with torch.co

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output — the agent has been stuck for 3 consecutive trials. With ~33 minutes remaining, providing exact copy-paste code as the final attempt. The approach is pure PyTorch with BN f
